alt: 图片展示了传统单体开发、传统团队与Agent团队在速度、专注、
图片图片描述专家图片展示了Agent的能力边界，分为Agent擅长的、人类擅长的、Agent不擅长的三部分。Agent擅长的包括代码、文档、测试、重复、搜索等任务；人类擅长的涵盖决策、创造、沟通、判断、分析等；Agent不擅长的有幻觉、长程、创造、伦理、审美等。该图与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了Agent在不同任务上的能力差异，帮助理解其在

图片展示了搭建Agent团队的真实成本。左侧为API/Token消耗，包括推理模型（Claude/GPT）0.15 - 9.0/M tokens、快速模型（Haiku/GPT - 4o - mini）0.03 - 0.15/M、Embedding / 向量0.02/M，以及向量数据库/存储、CI/CD / 部署的费用。右侧是时间投入，涵盖搭建期（2 - 4周）、前孵化期（1 - 2月）、后续孵化（每周2 - 4h）等阶段。底部有“$200 - 800”字样。该图与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了搭建Agent团队在成本方面的具体构成。

图片展示了搭建Agent团队的真实成本。API / Token消耗方面，推理模型（Claude/GPT）为$0.15 - $0.90/M tokens，箭头指向其0.15 - 0.90的数值；快速模型（Haiku/GPT - 4o - mini）为$0.03 - $0.15/M，箭头指向其0.03 - 0.15的数值；Embedding / 向量为$0.02/M，箭头指向其0.02的数值。基础设施中，向量数据库 / 存储为$20 - 200/月，CI / CD / 部署为$10 - 50/月。时间投入包括搭建期、前孵化期、后续孵化期。还列出了机会成本、隐性成本、试错成本等。

图片展示了“分工的艺术·Agent角色体系”，包含Coding Agent、Review Agent、Testing Agent三种角色。Coding Agent专职编码实现等；Review Agent负责代码审查等；Testing Agent执行测试全流程自动化等。各角色有明确边界，如Review Agent不碰代码只提建议，Testing Agent不决定测试策略。图片与上文运维Agent Rex越界修改Kai代码的案例相呼应，强调了明确Agent角色分工和边界的重要性。

图片展示了让Agent认识彼此的共享记忆架构。中心为共享记忆仓库（Git），包含structured - memory/、decisions/等目录，有git commit & push、git pull & merge操作。四周分布着Coding Agent、Reviewview Agent等不同Agent，分别负责写入、读取等任务。底部说明共享记忆为Git仓库持久化、Markdown数据结构化，Agent们在同一个知识空间协作。该图与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了文档中提到的Agent如何通过共享记忆进行协作。

alt: 图片 addCriterion图片图片展示了让Agent认识彼此的共享记忆架构。中心为共享记忆仓库（Git），包含structured-memory/、decisions/、progress/、context/、references/等目录，有git commit & push、git pull & merge操作。。周围是多个Agent，如Coding Agent、Review Agent、Testing Agent、Docs Agent、DevOps Agent、Supervisor、Research Agent、Future Agents，分别负责不同任务，如每次读写记忆、依赖记忆上下文、获取测试策略、提取变更、部署状态写入、全局视角协调、调研结果归档、记忆API等。图片与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了各Agent与共享记忆仓库的交互及分工协作情况。

图片展示了混合通信体系，分为CLI模式（结构化）、NL模式（自然语言）和混合模式。CLI模式适用于Agent间Routine交互、状态同步、任务分配等场景，优势为精准、可脚本化、低Token消耗等。NL模式适用需求讨论、Bug描述等场景，优势是表达丰富、隐含信息可传递等。混合模式是最佳实践，实现方式为CLI发送结构化请求 + NL附上下文说明等。图片与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了不同模式的特点及适用场景。
图片展示了混合通信体系，分为从左侧起分别为CLI模式（结构化）、NL模式（自然语言）和混合模式。CLI模式适用于Agent间Routine交互，状态同步、任务分配等ylko

图片展示了从等任务到主动提案的两种模式对比。左侧为被动模式，人类分配任务，Agent执行并完成，人Agent比例1:1，空闲时间占50%；右侧为主动模式，人类设定目标，Agent分析现状、提方案，人Agent比例1:5，空闲时间仅占10%。图片与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了Kai因等任务闲置10小时的被动状态，以及主动提案模式下Agent高效工作的优势。
图片展示了从等任务到主动提案的两种模式对比。左侧为被动模式，人类分配任务，Agent执行并完成，人Agent比例1:1，空闲时间占50%；右侧为主动模式，突出提案，人类设定目标，Agent分析现状、提方案，人Agent比例1:5，空闲时间仅占10%。该图与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了从被动执行到“等任务”到主动提案的转变，强调主动提案能有效减少空闲时间，提升效率。
图片展示了Agent自进化机制的四个阶段。阶段1为观察，通过执行任务记录成功/失败模式、Token消耗审计；阶段2为分析，识别流程模式、发现改进机会、对比历史表现；阶段3为改进，优化Prompt、调整工作流程、更新知识库；阶段4为验证，A/B测试验证、效果量化评估、迭代循环。每24小时一次自动进化循环，人类仅需审批改进提案。图片与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了Agent自进化的工作流程。

图片描述了“花时间 vs 花钱 · Cost vs Efficiency”的对比。人工实现重构模块需3 - 5天，编写测试用例1 - 2天，代码Review 2 - 4h，文档维护成本$2400/周，优势为质量可控、深度思考，但慢、耗时、易疲劳；Agent实现仅15分钟，30秒自动，优势为快、24h工作、可复制，但需Review、Token成本。核心公式为人类时间×时时薪 > Token成本 → 果断用Agent，强调50x速度提升vs 2%的成本。
图片对比对“花时间”和“花钱”两种方案在成本与效率上的差异。人工实现方面，重构模块需3 - 5天，编写测试用例1 - 2天，代码Reviewiew

图片展示了Agent故障模式与恢复流程。左侧为典型故障模式，包括编造不存在的API/函数、假代码，破坏源文件、修改不修改的代码，无限循环、同一个问题反复打转、无法跳出，随着做着忘了原始目标、越偏越远，一个Agent的错误引发连锁反应，失败后静默、不报告、假装成功，占比23%，平均发现时间为12min。右侧是恢复流程，包括检测、暂停受影响Agent、分析、修正、重试等步骤，94%的自动恢复率，平均恢复时间<5min。

图片“Agent搞砸了怎么办 · 故障 addCriterion故障模式与恢复”标题下，展示了Agent典型故障模式及恢复流程。故障模式包括编造不存在的API/函数、假代码，破坏源代码文件、修改不修改的代码，无限循环、同一个问题反复打转，无法跳出，随着做着忘了原始目标、越偏越远，一个Agent的错误引发连锁反应，失败后静默 addCriterion默、不报告、假装成功等，占比23%，平均发现时间为12min。恢复流程涵盖检测、暂停、分析、修正、验证、学习等步骤，94%可自动恢复，平均恢复时间<5min。

图片展示了多Agent协作协议栈的层次结构。从上至下依次为紧急协议、协商协议、状态协议、通信协议和任务任务协议。紧急协议用于处理无法处理的异常；协商协议是Agent间沟通协议；状态协议让每个Agent广播状态等；通信协议是Agent间消息传递的底层规范；任务协议是任务创建、分配、执行、完成的标准格式和流程。图片与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了Yason设计协议栈时借鉴的Claude Code Agent Teams架构。
图片展示了多Agent协作协议栈的层次结构。从上至下依次为紧急协议、协商协议、状态协议、通信协议和任务协议。紧急协议用于处理无法无法处理的异常；协商协议是Agent间沟通的协议；状态协议让每个Agent广播状态等；通信协议是消息传递的底层规范；任务协议是任务创建等的标准格式和流程。图片与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了协议栈各层

图片展示了子Agent架构，以“Supervisor Agent”为核心，周围分布着多个子Agent，如Review Agent、Testing Agent等，每个子Agent下方标注了数量，如Supervisor为1、Main Agent为4、子Agent总数为11。图片下方文字说明子Agent架构的威力在于“分而治之”——每个子Agent专注一个领域，组合起来就是全线团队。该图与上下文紧密相关，直观呈现了文档中提到的子Agent架构，帮助理解文档中Kai因上下文窗口不够而卡住的问题。
图片展示了子Agent架构，以“Supervisor Agent”为核心，周围分布着多个子Agent，如“Review Agent”“Testing Agent”等。每个子Agent下方标注了其数量，如“

图片展示了接入外部工具 - MCP集成架构。MCP协议层是所有Agent与外部世界对话的统一语言，连接一切。Agent Runtime负责工具发现、连接、调用、结果解析等。MCP Server负责工具注册、鉴权、限流、日志等。图中还列出了多个工具类别，如GitHub、数据库、云服务、项目管理、通信、向量DB等，以及监控、认证、文档、测试、容器、CI/CD、客服、AI模型等工具，直观呈现了MCP的集成架构。

图片展示了接入外部工具的MCP集成架构。MCP协议层是Agent与外部世界对话的统一语言，连接一切。Agent Runtime负责工具发现、调用、结果解析等。MCP Server管理工具注册、版本、期限、日志等。左侧有GitHub、数据库、云服务等工具，右侧有监控、认证、文档、测试等服务。底部文字强调MCP是所有Agent与外部世界对话的统一语言。该图与上下文介绍的MCP协议层、1. 用于统一Agent与工具的交互标准，实现Agent与工具的高效连接和协作。
图片展示了Agent自进化与自我修复的流程。从左至右依次为监控、诊断、修复、验证、学习。监控到异常后自动核心系统隔离，生成修复，验证通过后知识入库。还呈现了15min的平均检测时间、3min的自动修复时间、96%的自动修复成功率、87%的复发率降低等数据。底部提示自我修复的最高境界是不出错，而是出错了能自己修好，并且下次不再犯同样的错。

Diagram of "Writing personas for Agents · System Prompt anatomy" — shows the building blocks of a System Prompt: role, context, goals, constraints, style, output, workflow, etc. For example, the role is a senior frontend architect, the context is a project using a Monorepo architecture, and the goal is to implement features with minimal code changes. alt: Diagram of "Writing personas for Agents · System Prompt anatomy" — shows the structure of a System Prompt, broken down into role, context, goals, constraints, style, ...

Diagram showing the "Art of Division · Agent role system," including Coding Agent, Review Agent, and Testing Agent roles. The Coding Agent specializes in code implementation, etc.; the Review Agent handles code review, etc.; the Testing Agent runs the full test pipeline automatically, etc. Each role has clear boundaries — e.g., the Review Agent never touches code, only suggests; the Testing Agent doesn't decide test strategy. This diagram echoes the earlier case where Ops Agent Rex overstepped and modified Kai's code, underscoring the importance of clear Agent role division and boundaries.

alt: Diagram showing the Coding Agent, Review Agent, and Testing Agent in the Agent role system. At the Coding Agent, its responsibilities span code implementation and beyond — a full breakdown of each role's scope and boundaries within the team.

Diagram showing the shared-memory architecture that lets Agents know each other. At the center is a shared memory repo (Git), containing structured-memory/, decisions/, and other directories, with git commit & push and git pull & merge operations. Around it are different Agents like the Coding Agent and Review Agent, each responsible for writing, reading, etc. At the bottom it explains the shared memory is persisted as a Git repo with Markdown data structures, and the Agents collaborate in one shared knowledge space. This diagram is closely tied to the context, visually presenting how the Agents in the text collaborate through shared memory.

alt: Diagram showing the shared-memory architecture that lets Agents know each other. At the center is a shared memory repo (Git), containing structured-memory/, decisions/, progress/, context/, references/, and other directories, with git commit & push and git pull & merge operations. Around it are multiple Agents — Coding Agent, Review Agent, Testing Agent, Docs Agent, DevOps Agent, Supervisor, Research Agent, Future Agents — each responsible for different tasks, like reading/writing memory per interaction, depending on memory context, fetching test strategy, extracting changes, writing deploy status, coordinating from a global view, archiving research results, and memory APIs. The diagram is closely tied to the context, visually presenting how each Agent interacts with and collaborates around the shared memory repo.

Diagram showing the hybrid communication system, divided into CLI mode (structured), NL mode (natural language), and hybrid mode. CLI mode fits routine Agent interactions, status sync, task assignment, etc., with advantages like precision, scriptability, and low token cost. NL mode fits requirement discussions, bug descriptions, etc., with advantages like rich expression and transmissible implicit info. Hybrid mode is the best practice, implemented as CLI sending structured requests + NL attaching context explanations. The diagram is closely tied to the context, visually presenting the traits and use cases of the different modes.

Image showing a comparison of two modes — from waiting for tasks to proactive proposals. On the left is the passive mode: a human assigns tasks, the Agent executes and completes them, human-to-Agent ratio 1:1, idle time 50%. On the right is the proactive mode: a human sets the goal, the Agent analyzes the current state and proposes a plan, human-to-Agent ratio 1:5, idle time only 10%. The image is closely tied to the context, vividly showing Kai's passive state of sitting idle for 10 hours waiting for tasks, and the advantage of Agents working efficiently under the proactive-proposal mode.

alt: Image showing a comparison of two modes — from waiting for tasks to proactive proposals. On the left is the passive mode: a human assigns tasks, the Agent executes and completes them, human-to-Agent ratio 1:1, idle time 50%. On the right is the proactive mode, highlighting proposals: a human sets the goal, the Agent analyzes the current state and proposes a plan, human-to-Agent ratio 1:5, idle time only 10%. The image is closely tied to the context, vividly showing the shift from passive execution / "waiting for tasks" to proactive proposals, emphasizing that proactive proposals effectively cut idle time and boost efficiency.

Image showing the four stages of the Agent self-evolution mechanism. Stage 1 is Observation — recording success/failure patterns and auditing token consumption while executing tasks. Stage 2 is Analysis — identifying process patterns, discovering improvement opportunities, and comparing against historical performance. Stage 3 is Improvement — optimizing Prompts, adjusting workflows, and updating the knowledge base. Stage 4 is Verification — A/B test validation, quantified effect evaluation, and iterative looping. An automatic evolution loop runs every 24 hours, and the human only needs to approve improvement proposals. The image is closely tied to the context and vividly presents the Agent self-evolution workflow.

Image describes the comparison of "Spending Time vs Spending Money · Cost vs Efficiency". Manual refactoring of a module takes 3–5 days, writing test cases 1–2 days, code review 2–4h, documentation maintenance cost $2400/week; advantages are controllable quality and deep thinking, but it's slow, time-consuming, and prone to fatigue. The Agent approach takes only 15 minutes, 30 seconds automated, with advantages of being fast, working 24/7, and replicable, but requires review and has token costs. The core formula: human time × hourly rate > token cost → decisively use the Agent, emphasizing a 50x speed boost vs 2% cost.
context_heading: Cost to Efficiency — Spend Money, Not Time
next_line: ## The Bill That Made Yason Wince

alt: Image compares the cost and efficiency differences between the "spend time" and "spend money" approaches. On the manual side, refactoring a module takes 3–5 days, writing test cases 1–2 days, code review

Image shows Agent failure modes and recovery flow. On the left are typical failure modes, including making up non-existent APIs/functions, fake code; destroying source files, modifying code that shouldn't be changed; infinite loops, going in circles on the same problem, unable to break free; as it works it forgets the original goal, drifting further and further off; one Agent's error triggers a chain reaction; after failure it goes silent, doesn't report, pretends to succeed, accounting for 23%, average detection time 12min. On the right is the recovery flow, including detection, pausing the affected Agent, analysis, correction, retry, etc., with a 94% auto-recovery rate and average recovery time <5min.
context_heading: Crash Scenes — When Agents Mess Up
next_line: ## qclaw Started Up and Wouldn't Stop

alt: Image "What to do when your Agent messes up · failure modes and recovery". Under the title, it shows typical Agent failure modes and the recovery flowchart. Failure modes include making up non-existent APIs/functions, fake code; destroying source files, modifying code that shouldn't be changed; infinite loops, going in circles on the same problem, unable to break free; as it works it forgets the original goal, drifting further off; one Agent's error triggers a chain reaction; after failure it goes silent, doesn't report, pretends to succeed, etc., accounting for 23%, average detection time 12min. The recovery flow covers detection, pause, analysis, correction, verification, learning, etc., 94% auto-recoverable, average recovery time <5min.

Image showing the layered structure of the multi-Agent collaboration protocol stack. From top to bottom: emergency protocol, negotiation protocol, status protocol, communication protocol, and task protocol. The emergency protocol handles exceptions that cannot be processed; the negotiation protocol is the Agent-to-Agent communication protocol; the status protocol lets each Agent broadcast its status; the communication protocol is the underlying specification for message passing between Agents; the task protocol is the standard format and flow for task creation, assignment, execution, and completion. The image is closely tied to the context, visually illustrating the Claude Code Agent Teams architecture that Yason drew on when designing his protocol stack.

Image showing the layered structure of the multi-Agent collaboration protocol stack. From top to bottom: emergency protocol, negotiation protocol, status protocol, communication protocol, and task protocol. The emergency protocol handles unhandled exceptions; the negotiation protocol is the Agent-to-Agent communication protocol; the status protocol lets each Agent broadcast its status; the communication protocol is the underlying specification for message passing; the task protocol is the standard format and flow for task creation and so on. The image is closely tied to the context, visually presenting each layer of the protocol stack

Image showing external tool integration — the MCP integration architecture. The MCP protocol layer is the unified language through which all Agents talk to the outside world, connecting everything. The Agent Runtime handles tool discovery, connection, invocation, and result parsing. The MCP Server handles tool registration, auth, rate limiting, and logging. The diagram also lists multiple tool categories such as GitHub, database, cloud service, project management, communication, and vector DB, as well as monitoring, auth, docs, testing, container, CI/CD, customer service, and AI models — visually presenting the MCP integration architecture.

Image showing external tool integration — the MCP integration architecture. The MCP protocol layer is the unified language through which all Agents talk to the outside world, connecting everything. The Agent Runtime handles tool discovery, invocation, and result parsing. The MCP Server handles tool registration, versioning, expiry, and logging. On the left are tools like GitHub, database, and cloud service; on the right are monitoring, auth, docs, and testing services. The bottom text emphasizes that MCP is the unified language for all Agents talking to the outside world. The image is tied to the MCP protocol layer introduced in the context — 1. a unified standard for Agent-tool interaction, enabling efficient connection and collaboration between Agents and tools.

Image showing the Agent self-evolution and self-repair flow. From left to right: monitor, diagnose, repair, verify, learn. After an anomaly is detected, the core system is isolated automatically, a fix is generated, and once verification passes it's added to the knowledge base. It also shows data such as 15min average detection time, 3min auto-repair time, 96% auto-repair success rate, 87% recurrence reduction. The bottom note says the highest level of self-repair is not to never err, but to fix errors on your own and not repeat the same mistake next time.
context_heading: Self-Evolution and Self-Repair — Agent Teams That Grow Smarter
